F: Jina rerank-v2 cross-encoder; trained on query-document pairs; outputs raw logits (not calibrated probabilities) → apply sigmoid(raw) to normalize; F: DEDUP_RERANK_THRESHOLD = 0.55 (sigmoid-normalized); empirical: score ≥ 0.55 = duplicate, < 0.55 = noise; F: threshold defined in mnemos.constants

D: topic-level merge clusters (cosine 0.75–0.88) require stricter guard rails than near-duplicate clusters (>0.88) ∵ LLM over-generalizes & drops specifics on loose inputs; F: hierarchical binary merge (v10) mitigates via pairwise-only merging ∴ 'output ≈ one input size' intuition holds

D: qdrant API key rotation procedure; F: generate via `openssl rand -hex 32`; F: write to /etc/qdrant/config.yaml @ service.api_key; F: `systemctl restart qdrant`; F: update MNEMOS_QDRANT_API_KEY in /etc/mnemos/env; F: `systemctl restart mnemos-mcp`; F: verify via `curl -H 'api-key: NEW' http://localhost:6333/collections`; W: old key remains valid until rotation completes

per_memory.cml_output: D: vector store backend for Mnemos: stick with sqlite-vec as default; pgvector as optional backend ∵ zero-deployment is feature for single-user system; sqlite-vec: rock solid, zero deployment (embedded in same SQLite file as memory data), sufficient at current scale (no multi-node search needed); pgvector: already running Postgres for app data, but adds maintenance overhead; decision prioritizes simplicity over infrastructure consolidation

F: embedding pipeline (FastEmbed multilingual-e5-large) on Ryzen 7 8-core + 64GB ECC RAM; cold start load ~9s; resident RAM ~2.1GB post-load; inference throughput (batch 100 short passages) ~120 passages/sec; first memory_search post-startup ~15ms (model warm via MCP server warmup hook)
D: quote measured numbers precisely going forward ∵ had been citing inconsistent values across contexts
L: CPU-native ONNX runtime throughput better than expected
